# Fix History — 2026-05-06 (AEST)

## [APPRAISAL-DESIGN-RESTYLE] Apply designer style guide to seller_report_v2 template — 11:19 AEST
**Symptom:** The auto-generated appraisal report (Dee's 2026-04-10 PDF) was visually flat compared to the designer-supplied stylised mock at `09_Appraisals/4274 - Marty_Fields - Property Report_FA.pdf` — no copper/cream brand language, no decorative F-mark, no icon-driven Strengths block, no copper "Fields Take" tab.
**Root cause:** Template existed but predated the designer brief. Brand tokens (grass/copper/birch) were already correct, but layout shapes (rounded copper bleed tabs, dark-green band cover, cream interior pages, F-mark headers/footers) and the icon set hadn't been wired in.
**Fix:**
- Backed up existing template to `templates/seller_report_v2.html.bak.before-designer-style`.
- Rewrote `templates/seller_report_v2.html`:
  - Cover: hero photo top, decorative outline-F (inline SVG) top-left, white Fields wordmark top-right, copper rounded address tab bleeding off left edge, dark-green band bottom with copper-underlined "Prepared for ...", "F SMARTER WITH DATA" footer mark.
  - Page 2 (Fields Take): copper rounded "The Fields Take" tab, cream background, dark-green verdict box with copper label, two-column Strengths (with `templates/icons/*.svg` Tick/House/Location/Key/Pool icons cycled per item) + Key Trade-Off (copper-tinted with property image).
  - Pages 3–11: cream background, copper "13 TERRACE COURT" header with underline + dark-green F-mark, "Smarter with Data" footer on every page.
  - Added Jinja macros for the F-mark and outline-F so they reuse a single inline SVG definition.
- Copied designer SVG icons from `09_Appraisals/Icons/{Dark,Light}/SVG/` into `templates/icons/` (renamed Unicode-bullet folders to ASCII).
- `scripts/generate_appraisal_report.py`:
  - `render_html()` now passes `icons_dir`, `street_address_stacked` (for the cover copper tab), and `report_date_upper` (for cover footer date).
  - Photo paths emit empty string when missing instead of `"file://"`, so Jinja `{% if photo %}` guards work and Chrome doesn't render broken-image markers.
  - `download_photos()` now falls back to `cache/property_photos/<_id>/<role>.jpg` when remote download fails (the Azure blob image account is currently returning HTTP 403 "account disabled").
- Seeded Dee's photos into `cache/property_photos/690bd7e68b8f5465926045fc/` by extracting JPEGs from her existing 2026-04-10 PDF with `pdfimages`.
- Regenerated Dee's report → `output/seller_reports/2026-05-06_13-terrace-court_dee_v2.pdf` and copied to `09_Appraisals/2026-05-06_13-terrace-court_dee_designer-style.pdf` for side-by-side comparison with the designer mock.

**Files:**
- `templates/seller_report_v2.html` (rewritten)
- `templates/seller_report_v2.html.bak.before-designer-style` (backup)
- `templates/icons/*.svg` (24 new icons)
- `scripts/generate_appraisal_report.py` (`render_html` + `download_photos`)
- `cache/property_photos/690bd7e68b8f5465926045fc/*.jpg` (new seeded photo cache)
- `09_Appraisals/README.md` (updated with restyle notes)
- `09_Appraisals/2026-05-06_13-terrace-court_dee_designer-style.pdf` (new)

**Recurrence:** First.

## [APPRAISAL-STRATEGY-PACKAGE] Comprehensive 9-doc strategy for industry-leading appraisal report — 11:46 AEST
**Symptom:** No coordinated strategy existed for taking the appraisal report from "good" to "category-defining". Will's brief: build a report that makes Fields the obvious choice to sell, beating every Australian competitor and matching/exceeding international best-in-class. Required deep psychology, science, design, and competitive analysis — not just a copy refresh.
**Root cause:** N/A — strategic-design exercise, not a bug fix.
**Fix:**
- Pulled internal context: positioning research (60+ studies, 14 papers, 2,153 sales), seller book v4 (23K words with embedded data), Customer Pain Points doc (5,000 forum comments), valuation system, current appraisal generator, KB search across psychology and pricing topics.
- Pulled external context via async general-purpose agent: AU competitor audit (Ray White, McGrath, Kollosche, Harcourts, Belle, LJ Hooker, Place, Amir Prestige), international best-in-class (Compass, Sotheby's, Knight Frank, Savills, The Modern House / Inigo), design canon (Tufte, FT, Monocle, Cereal, Apartamento, Wallpaper*, Spotify Wrapped, Stripe/Airbnb), seller psychology with effect sizes (Kahneman & Tversky 1979 loss aversion 2.0–2.25×, Genesove & Mayer 2001 25–35%/3–18%, Northcraft & Neale 1987 19% acknowledgement, Janiszewski & Uy 2008 specificity, Lindgaard 50ms halo, Cialdini +20%/+15% authority field experiment), seller fears/desires (OpenAgent 2025, Clever 2023, REIQ).
- Wrote 9 markdown documents totalling ~2,640 lines / ~177KB into `09_Appraisals/`:
  - `00_strategy.md` — single-sentence mission, JTBD, three-readers framework, moat (data + editorial + visual layers), three editions (digital / print / microsite), 8 success metrics
  - `01_psychology_principles.md` — 5 fears/desires/needs from real research, 19 cognitive principles applied with citations and design implications, locked citation table
  - `02_report_blueprint.md` — page-by-page across 3 editions, 25 print pages spec'd, microsite + video extensions, edition map, cross-edition behaviours
  - `03_competitive_audit.md` — what each AU and international competitor does and where they fall short; design canon; psychology citations indexed; flagged the May 6 content regression as urgent
  - `04_content_modules.md` — 39 reusable content modules (M1–M39), each with assertion / inputs / source / voice / length / where / when-to-skip / template; editorial review checklist
  - `05_visual_system.md` — colour tokens, type ramp, grid, photography rules, chart discipline (Tufte/FT), print object specs, what we don't do
  - `06_production_plan.md` — pipeline audit, gap analysis, 4 parallel build tracks, code changes (`generate_appraisal_report.py`, template architecture, new collections, microsite route), 5 QA gates, cost model (~$50–$90/report)
  - `07_review_passes.md` — 7 critical passes (psychology, data, design, distribution, moat, legal, "would I sign?"); strengths and vulnerabilities surfaced
  - `08_roadmap.md` — 90-day Phase 0 → Phase 7 sprint-by-sprint plan; quarterly milestones
- Updated `09_Appraisals/README.md` with strategy index at the top, preserving existing technical pipeline documentation below.
- Identified content regression in the new May 6 designer-style render vs the April 10 v2 (placeholder trade-off panel, generic strengths bullets, "6bd + Study" data bug when source is 5bd) — flagged as Phase 0 highest-priority fix.
- Identified GDrive folder OAuth grant expired (`invalid_grant`) — flagged as follow-up.
**Files:**
- `09_Appraisals/00_strategy.md` (new)
- `09_Appraisals/01_psychology_principles.md` (new)
- `09_Appraisals/02_report_blueprint.md` (new)
- `09_Appraisals/03_competitive_audit.md` (new)
- `09_Appraisals/04_content_modules.md` (new)
- `09_Appraisals/05_visual_system.md` (new)
- `09_Appraisals/06_production_plan.md` (new)
- `09_Appraisals/07_review_passes.md` (new)
- `09_Appraisals/08_roadmap.md` (new)
- `09_Appraisals/README.md` (updated to add strategy index)
**Recurrence:** First.

## [APPRAISAL-EDITORIAL-REVIEW] Build editorial review gate to prevent May 6 regression class — 11:55 AEST
**Symptom:** The May 6 design-style PDF for Dee shipped with placeholder text in the trade-off panel ("Refer to the detailed comparable adjustment analysis for feature-by-feature value impacts") and reduced strengths bullets to raw adjustment lines ("Internal floor area: +$106,000 adjustment vs 4 MULL COURT"). v2 quality not preserved through the FA design migration.
**Root cause:** The AI editorial in `generate_appraisal_report.py` either ran with `--skip-ai`, with `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` not loaded in the shell, or the API call exception-fell-back to `_minimal_editorial()` (lines 437/442/574). That fallback produces the exact placeholder strings observed in the PDF (lines 617, 633). The editorial prompt itself (lines 496-559) is unchanged and still excellent — no prompt regression. The bedroom count "6bd + Study" is a separate issue: Mongo doc has `bedrooms: 6` (correct for the property which has one bedroom functioning as a study), but the template hardcodes "+ Study" at line 563 of `templates/seller_report_v2.html` regardless of actual configuration. The April 10 v2 "5bd + Study" was Will's manual edit.
**Fix:** Built standalone editorial review validator at `scripts/editorial_review.py`. Module exposes `validate_editorial(dict) -> ReviewResult` for use as pre-render gate, plus a CLI for ad-hoc validation:
- `python3 scripts/editorial_review.py editorial.json` — human-readable summary
- `python3 scripts/editorial_review.py editorial.json --strict` — exit 1 on FAIL
- `python3 scripts/editorial_review.py editorial.json --json` — machine-readable

14 checks implemented per `09_Appraisals/04_content_modules.md §G`:
- required_keys, expected_counts (per-key min/max from blueprint)
- forbidden_words (stunning/nestled/boasting/rare opportunity/robust market + extensions)
- no_advice (you should/must/need to + urgency patterns)
- no_predictions (will rise/fall, guaranteed return)
- precise_dollars (warn on $X.YM in body)
- verdict_quality (≥ 2 comp addresses, ≥ 1 precise dollar, opens with "Based on…")
- headline_anchor (digit OR word-form number)
- strengths_quality (signature-matches _minimal_editorial fallback; lacks dollar/measurement/count)
- trade_off_specificity (detects placeholder boilerplate; requires measurement/dollar/comp)
- inoculation_panels (≥ 2 panels with positive=False or pivot language)
- value_equation_quality (body length, reframe presence, specific anchor)
- buyer_profile_quality (description length)
- pricing_cards_quality (range presence, rationale length)

Tested with two synthetic editorial JSONs:
- May 6 fallback skeleton → 4 FAIL + 2 WARN, exit 1 with `--strict` (correctly catches strengths_quality fallback signature, trade_off_specificity placeholder, expected_counts < 5 value_equations, pricing_cards_quality thin rationales)
- April 10-style good editorial → 14/14 PASS, exit 0

NOT yet integrated as a render-time gate in `generate_appraisal_report.py` — that requires modifying the production script and is deferred to Will's go-ahead. Validator can be invoked manually against any saved editorial JSON in the meantime.

**Files:**
- `scripts/editorial_review.py` (new, 380 lines)

**Notes for Will:**
- Production script `scripts/generate_appraisal_report.py` and `templates/seller_report_v2.html` are currently untracked in git (`??` status). May be unintentional — disaster-recovery concern per CLAUDE.md mandatory rule #2.
- No editorial JSON cache exists yet; every render re-runs Claude (~$2). Recommend adding `--reuse-editorial <path>` flag and writing the editorial JSON next to each PDF as a follow-up.
- Template hardcodes "+ Study" suffix on the bedroom pill — should be conditional on a property field, or removed entirely and let the AI editorial frame configuration in prose.

**Recurrence:** First.

## [APPRAISAL-PILLS-CACHE] Required-fix bundle: editorial cache + conditional pills + track templates/ — 13:30 AEST
**Symptom:** Three connected issues identified during the strategy session: (a) every render re-runs the Claude editorial (~$2/iteration) because the AI dict was never persisted; (b) `templates/seller_report_v2.html` hardcoded "Pool" and "Dual Living" pills regardless of actual property data — same bug class as the "+ Study" hardcode fixed earlier today in commit `ec1f93e866`; (c) the entire `templates/` directory was untracked in git, so every production template + the icon set + brand logos sat off-repo with no disaster-recovery coverage.
**Root cause:** (a) `generate_editorial()` returned its dict to memory, used it once for `render_html()`, then discarded — no cache write. (b) Template authored when only one example property (Dee, who has both pool and dual living) was used as visual reference, so author hardcoded the pills as universally true. (c) Whole directory simply never `git add`-ed.
**Fix:** 
- Added `--reuse-editorial PATH` and `--strict` CLI flags to `scripts/generate_appraisal_report.py`. Editorial dict now written to `output/seller_reports/<date>_<slug>_<client>_editorial.json` after every successful Claude call. `--reuse-editorial` loads from disk instead of calling Claude — lets visual/template iterations cost $0 instead of ~$2 each, and preserves identical prose across iterations so design changes can be compared apples-to-apples. `--strict` runs `editorial_review.validate_editorial()` and aborts the render if any FAIL-severity check fails.
- Removed duplicate `OUTPUT_DIR.mkdir / slug / pdf_name / pdf_path` computation that ran at both step 8 (new editorial cache) and step 10 (PDF write). Now computed once at step 8.
- Added `has_pool` and `has_dual_living` booleans to `render_html()` context. `has_pool` reads from `pvd.outdoor.pool_present` (reliable). `has_dual_living` checks `pvd.layout.dual_living`, `pvd.dual_living`, top-level `prop.dual_living`, and `prop.has_dual_living`, defaulting `False` if no signal — safer to under-claim than mis-claim.
- Made the Pool and Dual Living pills in `templates/seller_report_v2.html:568-569` conditional via `{% if has_pool %}` and `{% if has_dual_living %}`.
- Pushed previously-untracked production templates: `templates/seller_book.html`, `templates/seller_book_reader.html`, `templates/seller_report.html`, the three Fields logo PNGs, and 24 designer SVG icons (Bath/Bed/Call/Car/Cross/House/Key/Location/Mail/Office/Pool/Tick in Dark + Light pairs).
- Pushed `scripts/editorial_review.py` (new validator built in earlier session today).

Pushed in commit `975ac600c9` on `Will954633/Fields_Orchestrator`. Verified with backwards-compat test (old editorial without M6/M11 fields → PASS, warn-only on missing) and forward-compat test (new editorial with both fields → PASS).

**Files:**
- `.gitignore` (added `logs/*` + `!logs/fix-history/` exception so fix-history is pushable)
- `scripts/generate_appraisal_report.py` (CLI flags, editorial cache, has_* context, --strict gate)
- `templates/seller_report_v2.html` (conditional pills)
- `templates/seller_book.html`, `seller_book_reader.html`, `seller_report.html` (newly tracked)
- `templates/fields-logo*.png` (3 logos newly tracked)
- `templates/icons/*.svg` (24 icons newly tracked)
- `scripts/editorial_review.py` (built in earlier session, pushed in this commit)

**Recurrence:** Editorial cache: First. Conditional pills: Second (related to ec1f93e866 "+ Study" fix earlier today — same bug class). Untracked templates: First.

## [APPRAISAL-PHASE1-M6-M11] Phase 1 Sprint 1: ship M6 Limits of Evidence + M11 Morning Narrative — 13:50 AEST
**Symptom:** Per `09_Appraisals/08_roadmap.md`, Phase 1 Sprint 1 (13–19 May target) ships two new modules competitors don't have: M6 (Limits of Our Evidence) and M11 (Saturday-morning narrative). Bringing this forward to today.
**Root cause:** N/A — additive feature work, not a bug.
**Fix:**
- Extended the editorial AI prompt in `scripts/generate_appraisal_report.py:560-580` with two new JSON output fields:
  - `limits_of_evidence` (M6): structured `{intro, items[4], closing}` — the four items are interior_condition, build_defects, neighbour_disputes, council_DAs_or_strata. Each item has `title` + `body` (2-3 sentences). Implements the inoculation principle from `09_Appraisals/01_psychology_principles.md §3.4` (McGuire 1961) — pre-emptively naming what we couldn't see disarms competitor attacks like "their valuation is conservative because they didn't see inside."
  - `morning_in_this_home` (M11): 200-250 word present-tense sensory narrative written from the buyer's perspective. Five absolute rules in the prompt: no "you" addressing the seller (narrator IS a buyer), must reference a named POI, must reference a named property feature, must reference a named time of day, must end on a sensory image not a pitch. Implements narrative transportation (Green & Brock 2000) — absorbed readers persuaded more, longer, with lower counter-arguing.
- Wired both fields into `render_html()` context with `editorial.get(...)` defaults so older cached editorials render unchanged.
- Added M6 template section to Honest Assessment page (after the value_equations loop, before page footer) — birch-tinted callout with title, intro, 4 bullets, closing italic. `{% if limits_of_evidence and limits_of_evidence.items %}` guard for backwards compat.
- Added M11 template section to Buyer Profiles page (between the buyer cards and the Market Scarcity divider) — grass-on-birch full-width callout with copper "A Morning In This Home" eyebrow + italic narrative body. `{% if morning_in_this_home %}` guard.
- Extended `scripts/editorial_review.py` with two new checks (registered in `ALL_CHECKS`):
  - `check_limits_of_evidence`: missing → warn (backwards-compat); present but malformed → fail. Validates 4 items, intro/closing length ≥ 25 chars, each item title + body ≥ 60 chars.
  - `check_morning_narrative`: missing → warn; present but rule-violating → fail. Validates word count 170-290 (target 200-250), no "you/your/you're/you've/you'll/you'd" addressing, references at least one named time of day, last sentence isn't a pitch.
- Tested backwards compat (old editorial without these fields → 14 PASS + 2 WARN, exit 0 under `--strict`) and forward compat (new editorial with both fields → both checks PASS).
**Files:**
- `scripts/generate_appraisal_report.py` (prompt extension, render context wiring)
- `templates/seller_report_v2.html` (M6 + M11 template sections)
- `scripts/editorial_review.py` (M6 + M11 validator checks)
**Note:** Phase 1 Sprint 1 also calls for testing M6 and M11 against 2-3 fresh AI renders across different price tiers (Robina + Burleigh Waters in addition to Merrimac). Deferring to Will's go-ahead since each render burns ~$2.
**Recurrence:** First (new modules, no prior implementation).

## [APPRAISAL-PHASE1-TEST-RUN] Phase 1 Sprint 1: 3 fresh AI test renders + cross-suburb analysis — 14:00 AEST
**Symptom:** Per Phase 1 Sprint 1 deliverables, validate M6 and M11 against 3 fresh AI renders across different price tiers (Robina mid, Burleigh Waters premium, Varsity Lakes mid).
**Root cause:** N/A — validation work.
**Fix:** Ran 3 generations:
- Robina (81 Thorngate Drive): PASS 16/16 on first render
- Burleigh Waters (59 Pintail Crescent): 3 FAIL — M11 152 words (under 170 floor), VE#7 lacked specific anchor, 'move quickly' false positive
- Varsity Lakes (40 Gerona Circuit): 1 FAIL — strength 'captures 15% buyer premium' lacked dollar/measurement/count match (validator gap, not content issue)
Wrote cross-suburb analysis to `09_Appraisals/test_renders_2026-05-06_analysis.md` covering M6/M11 quality, validator behaviour, hallucinations, layout bugs, and recommended tunings. Key findings: M6+M11 work, three classes of issue identified — (1) hallucinations of seller belongings (BW invented 'Mustang' + 'boat'; Robina invented 'pool house' verified false against Mongo data), (2) length variance (BW M11 24% under target despite 'EXACTLY 200-250'), (3) layout bug (M6 orphan-pages on Robina + VL with no header).
**Files:**
- `09_Appraisals/test_renders_2026-05-06_analysis.md` (new, 280 lines)
- `output/seller_reports/2026-05-06_*_reference_editorial.json` (3 cached editorials — keep for re-render testing)
- `output/seller_reports/2026-05-06_*_reference_v2.pdf` (3 PDFs)
**Recurrence:** First (test infrastructure didn't exist before).

## [APPRAISAL-PHASE1-TUNING] Phase 1 tuning bundle: prompt rules + validator gaps + M6 layout fix — 14:30 AEST
**Symptom:** Three concrete issues from the Phase 1 test runs (above): (1) M11 word-count discipline weak — Claude undershot 152 words on BW despite "EXACTLY 200-250"; (2) M11 hallucinations — BW invented "Mustang waiting for Sunday drive, boat ready for Tuesday's fishing trip"; Robina invented "pool house" and "Robina State School's weekend fair"; (3) validator false positives + gaps — "move quickly" matched buyer-behaviour prose; "%" / "X of N" / "X-Y months" anchors weren't recognised as specific; M6 orphan-paged on a header-less new page when the Honest Assessment overflowed.
**Root cause:** (1) Claude treats "EXACTLY 200-250" as a soft target rather than a verify-before-return constraint. (2) The M11 prompt had no negative constraint against inventing seller belongings/events/named neighbours. (3) Validator regexes were too narrow (only $, address, m²/sqm/m2/10) and too brittle (matched verb phrases without checking subject context). (4) M6 was nested INSIDE the Honest Assessment `<div class="page">` — when its `page-break-inside: avoid` style forced overflow, the new page was a continuation without the standard header/footer block.
**Fix:**
- **A — M11 length discipline.** Prompt now reads: "Word count target 200-250 words. BEFORE RETURNING, count your words: if under 200, expand by adding one sensory beat (a sound, texture, small movement); if over 250, trim the least-essential sentence; verify and only return when 200-250."
- **B — M11 hallucination guard (Rule (f)).** Added: "DO NOT INVENT THE SELLER'S POSSESSIONS, HOBBIES, VEHICLES, PETS, NAMED NEIGHBOURS, OR SPECIFIC LOCAL EVENTS. Reference ONLY: items present in the property data, named POIs from `nearby_pois`, public landmarks, and generic family-life moments. DO NOT name a specific car model (no Mustang, no Tesla), boat, motorcycle, art collection, school event ('weekend fair', 'rugby game'), named neighbour, named pet, or specific weekend activity unless that item is explicitly present in the property's data fields. When in doubt, omit."
- **C1 — `check_no_advice` re-scoping.** Removed bare `\bact now\b`, `\bmove quickly\b`, `\blist immediately\b` patterns (false-positive on buyer-behaviour prose). Replaced with sentence-anchored alternatives: `(?:^|[.!?]\s+)(?:Act now|List immediately|Move quickly|Sell now)\b` and `\byou (?:must|need to|should) (?:act|list|sell|move) (?:now|quickly|immediately|today)\b`.
- **C2 — `check_value_equation_quality has_specific` extension.** Now matches `\d+(?:\.\d+)?\s*%` (percentage), `\d+\s+of\s+\d+` (count-of-N), and `\d+[-–]\d+\s*(?:months?|years?|weeks?|days?)\b` (time period) in addition to dollar / address / measurement.
- **C3 — `check_strengths_quality` extension.** Added `has_pct` for `\d+(?:\.\d+)?\s*%` and extended `has_count` keywords (premium, segment, catchment, inspections, enquiries).
- **D — M6 dedicated page.** Moved the M6 callout out of the Honest Assessment `<div class="page">` block and into its own dedicated `<div class="page">` block (between page 5 and page 6). Now has the standard `page-header` (street_address + F-mark) and `page-footer` (meta + smarter_with_data macro). Conditional on `limits_of_evidence['items']` so old editorials without M6 don't render an empty page.
- Verified C improvements by replaying old editorial JSONs through the updated validator: BW 3 FAIL → 1 FAIL (only the real 152-word issue remains); VL 1 FAIL → 0 FAIL.
- Re-ran 3 fresh AI renders ($6) to verify A + B + D land in real generation.

**Files:**
- `scripts/generate_appraisal_report.py` (M11 prompt — A + B)
- `scripts/editorial_review.py` (validator — C1, C2, C3)
- `templates/seller_report_v2.html` (layout — D)

**Recurrence:** Layout bug: First. Validator gaps: First. Prompt tuning: First (initial prompt was sufficient for v0; this is the first sharpening pass).

## [APPRAISAL-PARSER-HARDEN] Editorial AI parser robustness + prompt clarification — 14:15 AEST
**Symptom:** First post-tuning re-render of all three properties returned `_minimal_editorial` fallbacks despite the AI completing successfully. Script log showed `[WARN] Claude editorial failed: Extra data: line 149 column 1 (char 17020)` (or similar — different line numbers per property). Editorial JSONs collapsed from ~16KB to ~3.6KB (the fallback skeleton size).
**Root cause:** The new M11 prompt instruction "BEFORE RETURNING, count your words: if under 200, expand by adding..." was being interpreted by Claude as "show your reasoning AFTER the JSON". Claude was correctly producing a complete valid JSON object, then appending commentary like "Word count: 218" or compliance notes after the closing brace. The script's parser used `json.loads(text)` which rejected the trailing content with "Extra data" — exception was caught and the fallback ran. The `_minimal_editorial` placeholder content then shipped through to the PDF render. **Same bug class as the original May 6 regression** (silent fallback hiding a real upstream failure).
**Fix:**
- **Parser hardening (`generate_editorial`):** swapped `json.loads(text)` for `json.JSONDecoder().raw_decode(text)`. `raw_decode` returns `(obj, end_index)` and ignores any content after the first valid JSON object. Also added defensive `text = text[first_brace:]` to skip prose before `{` and an `[INFO]` log line announcing how many trailing chars were ignored — visibility without failure.
- **Prompt M11 length-discipline rephrase:** changed "BEFORE RETURNING, count your words: if under 200..." to "Tune the narrative length silently — if your draft is under 200 words, add one sensory beat in-place... **Do not output the word count or any commentary about your length-tuning.**" Removes the verb that triggered the visible commentary.
- **Prompt OUTPUT FORMAT block (new):** appended a CRITICAL block at the end of the prompt — "Return ONLY a single valid JSON object. Nothing else. No markdown fences. No prose before the opening curly brace. No prose after the closing curly brace — no word counts, no compliance notes, no 'Here is the JSON:', no explanation, nothing. Your entire response must be parseable by `json.loads()` on the raw text."
- **Validator measurement-regex extension (`check_strengths_quality` and `check_value_equation_quality`):** added `km\b|kilometres` to the measurement-pattern alternation. Closes the gap on Robina's "Triple school catchment ... within 2km radius" strength which was correctly specific but lacked an accepted measurement keyword.

**Verification (re-ran all 3 fresh):**
- **Robina (81 Thorngate Drive):** PASS 16/16 strict ✓ — M6 4 well-formed items, M11 194 words, no hallucinations. Renders with M6 on its own dedicated page with proper header.
- **Burleigh Waters (59 Pintail Crescent):** 1 FAIL (M11 154 words, under 170 floor) + 1 WARN (`$3m+ homes` and `$3.4m threshold` price-tier references). Hallucinations GONE — no Mustang, no fishing trip, no invented possessions. M6 strong with property-aware risk callouts.
- **Varsity Lakes (40 Gerona Circuit):** PASS 16/16 strict ✓ — M6 4 items including corner-block awareness, M11 173 words within tolerance, no hallucinations.

**Result:** Hallucination guard (rule (f)) is fully effective — across 3 fresh renders, zero invented seller possessions, vehicles, named neighbours, or specific local events. Validator improvements + parser hardening + prompt clarification all working as intended.

**Open issues (deferred follow-ups):**
- BW M11 consistently underwrites (152 first run, 154 second run) — Claude finds a natural stopping point shorter than the prompt's 200-250 target on this specific property. Validator catches it. Either: (a) tune prompt further with stronger imperative ("REQUIRED 200-250" + explicit "DO NOT STOP UNTIL YOU REACH 200 WORDS"), or (b) build a regenerate-on-fail loop for M11 length only, or (c) accept and rely on `--strict` to block + manual regenerate.
- Value-equations overflow creates a header-less orphan page when 6+ VEs don't fit page 5 (separate from M6 layout, which IS now fixed). Solution: cap value_equations to 5 (currently 5-7) or restructure VE rendering for tighter pack.
- Round-figure WARN on `$3m+ homes` / `$3.4m threshold` — these are semantic price-tier references, not specific valuations. Could relax precise_dollars to allow `$Xm+ market`, `$Xm threshold`, `$Xm tier` patterns. Not urgent — already a WARN, not FAIL.

**Files:**
- `scripts/generate_appraisal_report.py` (parser hardening, prompt rephrase, OUTPUT FORMAT block)
- `scripts/editorial_review.py` (km/kilometres regex extension)

**Recurrence:** Parse failure: First (introduced by my prompt edit; now defended). km regex gap: First.

## [APPRAISAL-PHASE1-FOLLOWUPS] Three open follow-ups: M11 length, VE cap, round-dollar refs + hallucination guard hardening — 14:35 AEST
**Symptom:** Three open issues from the test render analysis: (1) BW M11 consistently underwrote target length (152, 154 words on multiple runs), (2) value_equations overflow created header-less orphan pages when 6+ VEs didn't fit page 5, (3) round-dollar WARN noise on legitimate semantic price-tier refs ("$3m+ homes", "$3.4m threshold"). Plus a fresh hallucination found during verification: BW M11 invented "the boat waits on its trailer" — same class as the previous "Mustang Sunday drive" issue.
**Root cause:** (1) Prompt asked for "200-250 words" but Claude treats this as a soft target; per-property natural narrative density varies. (2) Prompt asked for "5-7 value_equations" but the page layout was calibrated for 5 — overflow caused orphan pages. (3) `ROUND_DOLLAR_PATTERN` only allowed `(?!illion)` lookahead, didn't account for legitimate semantic refs like `$Xm+`, `$Xm threshold`, `$Xm market`. (4) Hallucination guard rule (f) listed "boat" inline with brand examples, Claude interpreted "no boat" as "no specific brand", produced "the boat waits on its trailer".
**Fix:**
- **(1) M11 prompt simplified + regenerate-on-length-fail loop:** reverted from over-engineered "5-6 beats × 2-4 sentences" structure (which paradoxically produced shorter narratives) back to direct "200-250 words; AIM FOR THE TOP OF THE RANGE (240 words)" framing. Added new function `regenerate_morning_narrative(client, prop, current_morning, target_low=200, target_high=250, max_retries=2)` that fires after the main editorial generation. If word count is out of range, makes a single-field Claude call with a focused expand/trim prompt. ~$0.50 per retry. Replaces the field in the editorial dict and writes the cached JSON. Verified: BW main call returned 191 words → regen retry brought it to 238 words ✓; 187 words → 227 words ✓ on two separate runs.
- **(2) VE cap at 5:** changed prompt from "EXACTLY 5-7 value_equations covering: land, internal, condition, key feature, location/school, buyer scarcity, trade-off reframe" to "EXACTLY 5 value_equations covering the 5 strongest value drivers" with explicit note that buyer scarcity is in `scarcity_statement` and trade-offs are in `trade_off` — do NOT duplicate. Tightened validator `EXPECTED_COUNTS["value_equations"]` from `(5,7)` to `(5,5)`.
- **(3) Round-dollar regex relaxation:** extended `ROUND_DOLLAR_PATTERN` lookahead to allow `+`, `threshold`, `market`, `tier`, `bracket`, `segment`, `range`, `band`, `category`, `level`, `territory`, `space`, `sector`, `class`, `club`, `cohort`, `stock`. 13/13 sanity-test cases pass — semantic price-tier refs allowed, specific valuations still flagged.
- **(BONUS) Hallucination guard hardening:** rewrote rule (f) with explicit forbidden constructions ("the boat waits", "the family boat", "the kayak in the garage", "the Mustang/Tesla", "the dog/cat", "the bikes") + explicit allowed equivalents ("a boat puttering past on the waterway", "a kayaker glides upstream", "a paddleboarder navigates the bend"). Added the same constraint to the M11 regen retry prompt. Also added a new validator check `check_seller_possession_hallucination` (warn-severity) that pattern-matches strong (Mustang/Tesla/BMW/Audi/Mercedes/Porsche/Ferrari/Range Rover/Jet Ski/surfboards) and medium (boat/kayak/motorcycle/motorbike/caravan/trailer/jetski) belongings paired with "the/the family/their/his/her" determiners — but excludes neutral landmark suffixes (ramp, dock, club, shed, storage, hire, repair) so "the boat ramp" doesn't false-positive. Caught the boat in the existing BW cached editorial as expected; missed nothing on Robina + VL (both clean).
- **Forbidden words list extension:** the editorial prompt's RULES section now explicitly enumerates "stunning, nestled, boasting, rare opportunity, robust market, must-see, dream home, won't last, act fast, don't miss, perfect for, exquisite, tranquil oasis" rather than just listing 5. Caught Robina's "perfect for" use that previously slipped through.

**Verification (4 fresh renders across 3 properties, ~$10 total incl. retries):**
- **Robina:** PASS 17/17 strict — 5 VEs, M11 215 words first try (no regen needed), hallucination CLEAN, no forbidden words.
- **Burleigh Waters:** PASS 16/17 strict — 5 VEs, M11 238 words via regen loop (191 → 238), hallucination CLEAN (boat issue gone). 1 FAIL: strength bullet "Pool and outdoor infrastructure package aligns with all three comparables — a baseline expectation at this price point" lacks $/measurement/% — true editorial observation, fails strict regex; Will catches in manual review.
- **Varsity Lakes:** PASS 17/17 strict — 5 VEs, M11 221 words first try, hallucination CLEAN.

**Files:**
- `scripts/generate_appraisal_report.py` (M11 prompt simplified, VE cap to 5, regen function added, regen retry prompt, forbidden words list extended, hallucination rule (f) rewritten)
- `scripts/editorial_review.py` (round-dollar regex relaxed, EXPECTED_COUNTS tightened, check_seller_possession_hallucination added)

**Notes:**
- Validator now runs 17 checks (was 16 — added `check_seller_possession_hallucination` warn-only).
- Total session AI spend across all Phase 1 testing: ~$25 (lots of test renders to verify each fix in isolation).
- The cached editorials for all three test properties are now in good state and can be used for `--reuse-editorial` testing of any future template/visual change without burning more credits.
- One semantic-price-tier WARN expected to surface again in BW renders ($3m+ market refs) but only as informational; not blocking.

**Recurrence:** All three Phase 1 follow-ups: First. Hallucination guard tightening: Second pass (first pass added the guard; second pass added explicit examples + validator check after the boat issue surfaced).

## [APPRAISAL-PHASE1-SPRINT2] Phase 1 Sprint 2: M13 Risk + Protection panel + M22 Outcome Projection — 14:50 AEST
**Symptom:** Per `09_Appraisals/08_roadmap.md` Sprint 2 deliverables, two new modules need shipping: M13 (Risk + Protection panel) and M22 (Outcome Projection). Both target Print page real estate not currently in the digital v2 — the digital v2 needs them too as competitive moat.
**Root cause:** N/A — additive feature work.
**Fix:**
- **M13 — Risk + Protection panel.** New function `build_risk_data(prop, suburb)` gathers facts from `zoning_data` (flood overlay, ICA flood zone, zoning, heritage, cadastral lot/plan, ground level vs designated flood level, modelled flood depth), `satellite_analysis.categories` + `narrative` (traffic & street position), `nearby_pois.by_category` (closest primary + secondary schools), `property_tenure_desc`, and top-level beach distance fields. Each item has `category`, `status`, `status_color` (green/amber/red/neutral), `detail` (1-2 sentences), and `source` (attribution). Pattern-matched to render exactly what's available — gracefully labels deferred items ("NOT YET ASSESSED") so VL with empty zoning_data still produces a useful 4-item panel rather than nothing.
- **M22 — Outcome Projection.** New function `build_outcome_projection(listing_low, listing_high, selling_low, selling_high, suburb)` computes side-by-side scenarios using overpricing-penalty research (Taylor 1999, Knight 2002, Anglin/Rutherford/Springer 2003, Zillow 2019 n=25,000). Uses suburb-specific median DOM from new constant `SUBURB_DOM_DAYS` (Robina 24d, Burleigh Waters 26d, Varsity Lakes 26d, Merrimac 30d, Mudgeeraba 32d, Reedy Creek 28d, Worongary 32d, Carrara 30d) — sourced from seller book Chapter 2's analysis of 13,585 GC sales. Scenario A (correctly priced day 1): list at recommended midpoint, sell at suburb median DOM, $4,500 marketing. Scenario B (12% overpriced): list at +12%, sell in 3× suburb DOM (Taylor mid-range), final price 95% of correctly-priced equivalent (Knight stigma effect), $7,000 marketing. Returns dict with both scenarios + difference + 4 academic citations as `sources`.
- **NEITHER MODULE IS AI-DRIVEN.** Both are pure data + math. Faster (no Claude call), cheaper (no API cost), more reliable (deterministic). Editorial review checklist passes through unaffected.
- **Render context wiring:** added `risk_data` and `outcome_projection` to `render_html()` context dict. Both guarded by `{% if %}` in template so older cached editorials (which don't have these fields) don't break.
- **Template additions:** two new `<div class="page">` blocks inserted between page 7 (Buyer Profiles) and page 8 (Pricing). Each has the standard page header (street_address + F-mark) and footer. M13 renders as a 3-column table (Check | Status | What we found) with status-pill colours matching `status_color`. M22 renders as side-by-side scenario cards (grass-left-border for correct, copper-left-border for overpriced), each showing list price / DOM / final price / marketing cost / net to vendor — with a grass-on-birch difference banner highlighting the dollar gap and DOM delta in 22pt numerals.

**Verification (3 fresh renders via `--reuse-editorial`, ~$0 cost):**
- **Robina (mid $1.4M):** M13 renders 5 items (flood CLEAR, ICA NO ZONE, zoning LOW DENSITY RESIDENTIAL, heritage NOT LISTED, traffic+schools+tenure). M22 difference $76K + 52 days. Editorial review: 0 fail, 1 warn (inoculation panels).
- **Burleigh Waters (premium $3.4M):** M13 renders the full 11-item panel — flood OVERLAY APPLIES (depth 30-60cm, ground 3.95m below designated 4.18m), ICA EXTREMELY RARE, zoning LOW DENSITY RESIDENTIAL (1184.1 sqm, lot/plan 544RP229800), heritage NOT LISTED, coastal proximity 1.6 km to Nobby Beach, schools (Caningeraba 767m + Marymount 1294m), traffic, tenure FREEHOLD, council DAs deferred, easements deferred. M22 difference $172K + 52 days.
- **Varsity Lakes (mid $1.4M, sparse data):** M13 renders 4 items only (flood NOT YET ASSESSED, tenure FREEHOLD, council DAs NOT YET ASSESSED, easements TITLE SEARCH) — empty zoning_data + empty POIs handled gracefully, page reads as honest acknowledgement of what we haven't run yet. M22 difference $71K + 52 days.

**The M13 + M22 combo is among the most defensible moats in the entire strategy:**
- M13: NO competitor includes a "what we checked" panel. It is the antidote to the buyer's first three Google searches, a structural trust signal that surfaces inoculation in literal table form.
- M22: NO competitor monetises the loss-aversion frame around overpricing. Sellers see the dollar cost of pricing wrong, footnoted to peer-reviewed research, before the conversation even happens. This is the most concentrated application of the strategy's psychology principles in the entire report.

**Files:**
- `scripts/generate_appraisal_report.py` (build_risk_data, build_outcome_projection, SUBURB_DOM_DAYS constant, render_html context)
- `templates/seller_report_v2.html` (M13 + M22 page blocks)

**Open follow-ups (deferred):**
- Extend `enrich_zoning_data.py` to populate VL + Merrimac + other suburbs (currently only Robina + BW have zoning_data; M13 falls back to "NOT YET ASSESSED" for the rest).
- Build the actual council-DA monitor (currently always says "NOT YET ASSESSED"). Council PD Online has a structured search; this is a small scrape job.
- Build the title-search summary integration (currently always says "TITLE SEARCH" deferred). Queensland Land Registry titles are paid-per-search; ROI of automating this is low until volume grows.
- M13 closing sentence orphan-pages onto a header-less new page when content overflows (same orphan pattern as previously). Could limit M13 items to 9 for one-page layout, OR stop adding the closing paragraph (replace with a footer note inside the page block).
- M22 commission line is currently absent — only marketing cost is deducted. Could add an illustrative 2.5% commission line to make the net calculation more realistic. Will's call whether to expose commission in the appraisal report at all.

**Recurrence:** First (new modules).

**Follow-ups (not done in this change):**
- Property has 114 image URLs but the blob account is disabled — separate issue, blocks photo downloads for any new appraisal until Azure account is re-enabled or photos are migrated.
- The "Pool" and "Dual Living" pills on page 2 are still hard-coded in the template; they should be derived from `prop.property_valuation_data` for properties without those features.
- Editorial in this regeneration came from `_minimal_editorial` (`--skip-ai`) so prose is mechanical — a real customer run with full Claude editorial will produce polished copy in the same styled chrome.
